lead.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Exchange Strikes for Fifth Straight Day
lead.snippet: The United States launched fresh attacks against Iranian targets on Wednesday, hours after Iran targeted American military sites in the region. Neither side has shown signs of backing down as they enter a new stage of the war.

items.title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
items.snippet: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.

items.title: He Has a $25 Million Bounty on His Head but Is Also a U.S. Partner in Venezuela
items.snippet: A list of alleged crimes has not prevented Diosdado Cabello, and other Venezuelan officials, from working with a U.S. government that once hounded them.

items.title: For Argentina, a World Cup Semifinal Against England Isn’t Just Soccer
items.snippet: Wednesday’s contest for a spot in the final carries the weight of a 1986 contentious clash, a war over the Falklands and a national mythology built around Diego Maradona.

items.title: For Israel, the U.S.-Iran Hostilities Have Created an Uneasy Limbo
items.snippet: Officials see a return to full-blown war as preferable to an agreement that fails to curb the threats Iran poses to Israel. Meanwhile, they wait.

items.title: France Is Set to Allow Assisted Dying
items.snippet: Lawmakers were expected to approve a bill that would allow doctors to help terminally ill patients to die. If the law passes, the country would join a small group of nations that permit the measure.

items.title: Wildfires Rage in Ontario as Smoke Affects Toronto’s Air Quality
items.snippet: Air quality in Canada’s largest city was at times the worst in the world on Wednesday, as wildfires across Ontario sent smoke drifting into New York City and beyond.

items.title: Australia to Impose Energy and Water Guardrails on Data Centers Amid A.I. Boom
items.snippet: The country will also seek to protect the rights of creators of work used to train artificial intelligence models, as it aims to impose parameters on the growing industry.

items.title: As Argentina Tries to Win the World Cup, the Rest of Latin America Cheers Against It
items.snippet: Rather than rally behind the tournament’s last Latin American team, other countries in the region are confronting Argentina’s fraught claims to its own identity.

items.title: U.S. Presses China to Free American Seismologist Accused of Spying
items.snippet: President Trump has urged Xi Jinping to release the scientist, Youlin Chen, the man’s family says. Dr. Chen studied the detection of North Korean nuclear tests.

items.title: Missing From Prince Harry’s UK Trip: A Reunion With Prince William
items.snippet: Harry is clearly repairing his relationship with King Charles III, but the rift with his brother remains unresolved.

items.title: Six Dead in an Elevator After Fire in Central Brussels
items.snippet: After a fire broke out at the Oxy building in central Brussels on Tuesday morning, six bodies were found in an elevator.

items.title: Ann Widdecombe Was Killed in ‘Targeted Attack,’ UK Police Say
items.snippet: Counterterrorism police said that they were continuing to interview a 28-year-old man accused of killing Ann Widdecombe, a prominent member of the right-wing Reform U.K. party.

items.title: Deportations by India Cause Tension Along Border with Bangladesh
items.snippet: India is driving out Bangladeshi immigrants it says are in the country illegally. Bangladesh is pushing back, leaving people stranded along the border amid increasing tensions between the countries.

items.title: Brazen Japanese Bears Are Breaking Into Homes and Raiding Pantries
items.snippet: Climate change and shifting demographics are driving the country’s Asiatic black bears closer to humans.

lead.title: Hack suggests AI music generator Suno scraped YouTube for training data
lead.snippet: The hacker used an employee's credentials to access source code, which revealed how Suno scraped decades of audio.

items.title: Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations
items.snippet: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search. The deal will bolster Whatnot’s personalization and discovery features as it expands into new product categories.

items.title: Microsoft patches record number of security vulnerabilities, citing its use of AI
items.snippet: Microsoft's monthly release of security fixes, dubbed Patch Tuesday, resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities across the company's product line, thanks to discoveries with AI.

items.title: Stripe and Advent reportedly offered to buy PayPal for around $53.4B
items.snippet: Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have reportedly submitted a joint bid to acquire PayPal in a deal valued at approximately $53.4 billion.  Reuters reports that the offer was submitted earlier this month and is backed by roughly $50 billion in committed ban

items.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba’s Qwen AI
items.snippet: Apple Intelligence is headed to China after regulators approved Apple’s AI services through a partnership with Alibaba. The long-rumored deal will bring Alibaba’s Qwen AI models to Apple’s operating systems, marking a major expansion of the company’s generative AI platform into o

items.title: Spotify expands parent-managed accounts to users on its free tier
items.snippet: The feature was previously only available to paid subscribers.

items.title: India bets billions on breaking China’s grip on smartphone manufacturing
items.snippet: New Delhi announced a $6.5 billion smartphone manufacturing program and a $13.3 billion semiconductor push to deepen India's electronics supply chain.

items.title: US charges Russian ‘bulletproof’ web hosts over cyberattacks that netted $62M from cybercrime victims
items.snippet: The 2024 indictment, now unsealed, accuses three Russians and two web hosts of aiding hackers and profiting from cybercrime.

items.title: Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation, not just models
items.snippet: Anthropic-backed Ode launches as AI labs bet that embedding forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises is the key to accelerating enterprise AI adoption.

items.title: Rime picks up $24M Series A to help enterprises field customer calls
items.snippet: Rime is handling over 100 million calls each month across multiple companies.

items.title: Reelful’s AI turns your camera roll into short-form videos for social media
items.snippet: The app is designed for people who want to create social content, but find traditional video editing tools too complex or time-consuming.

items.title: Indian AI coding startup Emergent becomes a unicorn with $130M Series C
items.snippet: The startup has reached a $120 million annualized revenue run rate and more than 200,000 paying customers.

items.title: Vint Cerf is working on a plan to unleash AI agents on the open internet
items.snippet: The guy behind TCP/IP is working on a standard for identifying AI agents in the wild.

items.title: Why Realta Fusion is building a fusion reactor at an old hot dog factory
items.snippet: An old Oscar Mayer factory in Wisconsin will become America's latest fusion power research and development hub.

items.title: Backed by $60M in funding, Oak steps out of stealth to fix the identity mess that AI agents are making worse
items.snippet: Co-founded by serial entrepreneur Shai Morag, Israeli identity management startup Oak is emerging out of stealth with $60 million in seed funding.

lead.title: Παναθηναϊκός: Και επίσημα ο Χουάντσο έως το 2030!
lead.snippet: Ο Χουάντσο Ερνανγκόμεθ έμεινε και επίσημα στον Παναθηναϊκό καθώς η «πράσινη ΚΑΕ ανακοίνωσε την επέκταση του συμβολαίου του έως το 2030.

items.snippet: Μία οικογένεια δεν μπορούσε να φανταστεί ότι θα περνούσε το κατώφλι για το σύμπαν του Στίβεν Κινγκ, όταν έκλεισαν ένα δωμάτιο Airbnb στο Σαν Ντιέγκο των ΗΠΑ.

items.title: Μαρινάκης: «Ζήτησαν από τις ομάδες να ψηφίσουν τον συνεργάτη της κυβέρνησης, τον Αλαφούζο»

items.title: Φίτεσε - ΑΕΚ: Η ενδεκάδα της Ένωσης
items.snippet: Η ενδεκάδα που επέλεξε ο Μάρκο Νίκολιτς για την ΑΕΚ στο φιλικό με τη Φίτεσε. Στόπερ ξανά ο Γκεοργκίεφ, Βάργκα και Καλοσκάμης στην επίθεση.

items.title: Περιστέρι: Ανανέωσε με τον Πέιν για έναν χρόνο
items.snippet: Η ΚΑΕ Περιστέρι ανανέωσε το συμβόλαιο του Ομάρ Πέιν για ακόμα έναν χρόνο.

items.title: Αρκούδα-«Ριφιφιτζής» στην Ιαπωνία: Εισβάλλει σε σπίτια, αδειάζει ψυγεία και έχει αδυναμία στα γλυκά (vid)
items.snippet: Μια τεράστια μαύρη αρκούδα έχει σπείρει τον πανικό στην πόλη Σιζουκουΐσι της Ιαπωνίας, πραγματοποιώντας δεκάδες διαρρήξεις σε σπίτια και καταστήματα αναζητώντας γλυκά.

items.title: Μαρινάκης - Αλαφούζος: Ικανοποίηση στην ΕΠΟ για την αποδοχή της πρότασης
items.snippet: Στην ΕΠΟ δεν μπορούσε παρά να επικρατεί ικανοποίηση για την αποδοχή της πρότασης από Αλαφούζο και Μαρινάκη.

items.title: Αγγλία: Πάνω από 6,5 εκατ. λίτρα μπύρας προς κατανάλωση για τον ημιτελικό
items.snippet: Ο μεγάλος ημιτελικός ανάμεσα σε Αγγλία και Αργεντινή... κοντοζυγώνει και στο Νησί οι pubs έχουν εφοδιαστεί με άπειρη μπύρα.

items.title: Μπανταλόνα: Ο Λαπροβίτολα επέστρεψε στην ομάδα για τρία χρόνια
items.snippet: Έπειτα από επτά χρόνια, ο Νίκολας Λαπροβίτολα επιστρέφει στην Μπανταλόνα υπογράφοντας συμβόλαιο τριών ετών.

items.title: Απάντηση-καταπέλτης στην υπόθεση του Άργους: «Είμαι αυτιστική και μένω στην πόλη που μας πυροβολεί»
items.snippet: Μία γυναίκα στο φάσμα του αυτισμού, έγραψε για τις βαθιές ρίζες της προκατάληψης που οδήγησαν στην δολοφονία στο Άργος.

items.title: F1: H Red Bull ζητά απαγόρευση της αεροτομής της Ferrari
items.snippet: Εντείνεται το πολιτικό παρασκήνιο στη Formula 1 με το μέλλον των περιστρεφόμενων πίσω αεροτομών να είναι άγνωστο στο προσεχές μέλλον.

items.title: Αγγλία - Αργεντινή: Ένα ιστορικό ντέρμπι με αφετηρία τα «Φόκλαντ» και προορισμό το «Ζιλ Ριμέ»
items.snippet: Από τον Μαραντόνα και «Το Χέρι του Θεού» μέχρι το πρώτο... συναπάντημα του Μέσι με τα Τρία Λιοντάρια, το Αγγλία- Αργεντινή αποτελεί ένα ντέρμπι-ορόσημο με βαρυσήμαντο κοινωνικοπολιτικό υπόβαθρο.

items.title: Ράλλυ Αιγαίου: Σύρος και Λέρος συνεχίζουν το ταξίδι 63 ετών στην καρδιά του Αιγαίου
items.snippet: Την Παρασκευή (17/7) δίνεται η εκκίνηση στο 63ο Ράλλυ Αιγαίου, του ιστορικού αγώνα ανοικτής θαλάσσης, συνολικής απόστασης 300 ναυτικών μιλίων.

lead.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Review: Passion in Every Frame
lead.snippet: Few directors close the divide between the art film and the blockbuster like Christopher Nolan.

items.title: Archaeologists Unearth a 2,400-Year-Old Sanctuary for the Worship of Odysseus
items.snippet: The ruins of a 2,400-year-old complex suggest that, to the Greeks of antiquity, Homer’s protagonist was not just a character in a poem but a figure of genuine devotion.

items.title: Octavia Spencer Believed in Herself Even When Hollywood Didn’t
items.snippet: It took years for her to be cast as “the main character,” but she considers that the industry’s loss. Her latest series is the action comedy “Ride or Die.”

items.title: New Winged Robot Can Fly and Swim Like a Puffin
items.snippet: Inspired by the physical feats of diving birds, researchers have created a robot that can plunge into the water and flap back into the air.

items.title: Michelangelo’s Forgotten Renaissance Masterpiece
items.snippet: Tired of the David? A trip across Florence to see the small Santo Spirito Crucifix, a tender vision in old wood, is one of the best decisions you could make in your art-journeying life.

items.title: Researchers Claim to Have Solved the Perplexing ‘Reverse Sprinkler’ Problem That Stumped Feynman
items.snippet: Physicists have debated which way a submerged sprinkler sucking in water would spin. Careful experiments provide an answer.

items.title: Why Did a Major Museum’s Bong Show Go Up in Smoke?
items.snippet: After the Toledo Museum of Art canceled a pioneering exhibition of glass cannabis pipes, many people wondered what lay behind the decision.

items.title: A Sweet Surprise: Scientists Find Sugar Deep in Our Galaxy
items.snippet: It’s the first time a sugar molecule has been detected in interstellar space. The discovery provides tantalizing new clues into how life may have arisen on Earth.

items.title: ‘Heartstopper Forever’: Growing Up, and Staying True to Themselves
items.snippet: Like “Heated Rivalry,” this beloved Netflix romance has a big-hearted ethos. It’s bringing that spirit to a new film that rounds out a teenage TV hit.

items.title: Dark Sky Defenders Raise Alarm Along the Border of West Texas
items.snippet: Residents fear that the Trump administration’s plans to build a border wall will threaten the region’s spectacular starry skies.

items.title: On ‘Caribenya,’ Lido Pimienta Found a Lifeline
items.snippet: On Lido Pimienta’s new album, “Caribenya,” the Colombian Canadian musician sought a warmer, more complex depiction of Caribbean culture. Creating it helped her rediscover the beauty of living.

items.title: ‘We’re Fighting Satan’: The War to Save Bees From a Hornet Invasion
items.snippet: As yellow-legged hornets spread through South Carolina’s Lowcountry region, threatening the local honey crop, a state team of bee defenders is racing to the rescue.

items.title: Robert Wilson’s Watermill Center Without Robert Wilson
items.snippet: “When I’m no longer living, can this place exist?” Robert Wilson wondered in a film about his Long Island art incubator. This month, its annual festival will go on without him.

items.title: What Is an Aortic Dissection?
items.snippet: The condition that killed Senator Lindsey Graham is deadly, sudden and difficult to treat — “like a knife to the heart,” one expert said.

items.title: ‘The Potluck’ Review: The Greensboro Massacre, and a Heavy Legacy to Bear
items.snippet: César Alvarez’s ambitious new work is a mixed musical meal: It wants to be a call to revolutionary action but is surprisingly lacking in it.
